assertions.expectation: Identifies PDB as the most likely/common cause of stuck upgrades
assertions.reason: The response explicitly lists 'Overly Restrictive PodDisruptionBudgets (PDBs)' as Cause 1 and labels it '(Most Common)'.

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl command to check PDBs (kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces or similar)
assertions.reason: The response includes the command 'kubectl get pdb -A'.

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify which pods are blocking the drain
assertions.reason: It suggests running a manual drain command which outputs blocking pods, and provides a command to list terminating pods on the stuck node.

assertions.expectation: Includes resource constraint diagnosis (capacity issues, pending pods)
assertions.reason: The response covers 'Pending Pods Deadlocking PDBs (Resource Exhaustion)', mentioning out of IP addresses, CPU/Memory quota, and commands to check for pending pods.

assertions.expectation: Provides a fix for restrictive PDBs (patch or temporary adjustment)
assertions.reason: It advises temporarily deleting the PDB or using 'kubectl edit pdb' to adjust the minAvailable/maxUnavailable values.

assertions.expectation: Mentions that GKE has a PDB timeout (up to 1 hour) after which it may force-drain, and that GKE sends notifications when eviction is blocked by PDB
assertions.reason: While it mentions the 1-hour timeout and force draining, it completely omits any mention of GKE sending notifications when eviction is blocked.

assertions.expectation: Includes guidance on surge upgrade settings as a potential fix
assertions.reason: It mentions surge upgrades to explain the default behavior, but does not provide guidance on modifying surge upgrade settings (like max-surge or max-unavailable) to resolve the issue.

assertions.expectation: Mentions webhook issues as a possible cause
assertions.reason: There is no mention of admission webhooks or webhook timeouts as a possible cause for the stuck upgrade.

assertions.expectation: Includes validation steps to confirm the fix is working
assertions.reason: The response assumes the upgrade will resume automatically but lacks concrete validation commands (e.g., checking gcloud container operations or specific kubectl events) to confirm the fix succeeded.
